package handler

import (
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for package_level_mutable_config_used_by_handlers_services.
// Rule intent: Mutable package globals used as live config in request paths,
// causing race conditions and unpredictable behavior under concurrent requests.

// Config is a package-level mutable map that handlers read and write during requests.
// This is the anti-pattern: shared mutable state at package scope.
var Config = map[string]string{
	"database_url":   "postgres://localhost:5432/mydb",
	"redis_url":      "redis://localhost:6379",
	"api_timeout":    "30s",
	"max_retries":    "3",
	"feature_flag_a": "off",
}

// Settings is another mutable package-level config struct.
var Settings = struct {
	DebugMode     bool
	LogLevel      string
	MaxConnections int
	RateLimit     int
}{
	DebugMode:      false,
	LogLevel:       "info",
	MaxConnections: 100,
	RateLimit:      1000,
}

type OrderHandler struct{}

func (h *OrderHandler) HandleOrder(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	// Anti-pattern: mutating package-level config during request handling
	Config["api_timeout"] = r.URL.Query().Get("timeout")
	Config["feature_flag_a"] = "on"

	if Settings.DebugMode {
		Settings.LogLevel = "debug"
	}

	_ = Config["database_url"]
}

type PaymentHandler struct{}

func (h *PaymentHandler) HandlePayment(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	// Anti-pattern: another handler mutating the same shared config
	Config["max_retries"] = "5"
	Settings.MaxConnections = 200
	Settings.RateLimit = 500

	_ = Config["redis_url"]
}
